mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% on 2020-01-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during easing periods do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% on 2020-01-01 near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. When both series decline simultaneously by > 2% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative due to earnings revision risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1608.0k on 2019-12-01 shows recent strength
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1% over a 2-month window, signaling supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces equity valuations. This mechanism triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.2026 on 2020-01-01 reflects pre-pandemic production levels
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns during supply contraction periods do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness. This threshold-triggered condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100k jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152186.0k on 2020-01-01 reflects strong pre-pandemic employment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100k, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, a monetary policy lag emerges. This interaction between loose monetary policy and rising inflation produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real rates compress and equity risk premiums decline.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.82 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% on 2020-01-01
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or mean returns during co-triggered periods do not exceed baseline by 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle weakness that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. This regime-specific mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days only when the economy is in the late-expansion phase (unemployment below 4.5%).
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19219.767B and UNRATE at 3.6% on 2020-01-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle deceleration instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regime-conditional regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 15% from their 12-month moving average, signaling a demand shock in the residential sector that propagates to broader economic weakness. This always-on mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as construction employment and consumer wealth effects deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1608.0k on 2019-12-01 shows recent housing market activity
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during housing decline periods do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
